jobDistinction.legacyPausedAuditJob: Broad website issue discovery plus small automatic fixes/upgrades; currently paused.
jobDistinction.currentRemediationJob: Backlog-driven remediation, optimization, and upgrade loop based on .hermes/remediation-backlog.md.

nonOverlapPolicy: If the previous remediation run is still active, skip the current tick instead of starting another fix run.

freshAuditPolicy.action: Run comprehensive audit, update remediation-backlog.md with newly discovered or reprioritized issues, then continue remediation.

lastHealthyBaseline.tests: Operation Task service/client regressions passed: 3 tests
lastHealthyBaseline.production: vcontrolhub-next and vcontrolhub-ssh-ws restarted active; /api/status healthy; operation task/requests/deployment probes returned 401/307/200; recent service logs clean

lastRun.target: Manual comprehensive audit across backend/API, frontend/UI, production behavior, performance, tests, deployment, and logs
lastRun.summary: Manual audit completed after the high-priority backlog became effectively complete. Production services and route probes were healthy, full quality gate passed, and new P1/P2/P3 findings were recorded in remediation-backlog.md for the next backlog-driven runs.
validation: paused current remediation cron job b8833e83bb1f to avoid overlap
git status and recent history checked
production services vcontrolhub-next, vcontrolhub-ssh-ws, and caddy active
major unauthenticated page/API probes returned 307/401/200 without 500
recent production logs checked with no error/warn matches
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test, npm run build, npm run build:runtime passed
remediation-backlog.md refreshed with new manual audit findings
lastRun.deployment: No code deployment performed; this run refreshed the remediation checklist only.
lastRun.nextTarget: Start with P1 audit findings: server-side guards for client-only pages, global search/navigation completeness, preference/refresh consistency, and SFTP sync/task UX.

runPolicy.batchSize: coherent deployable batch; may be larger for high-value architecture/performance/availability/user-experience improvements if fully verified
